steps.title: Identify the contrast signal
steps.content: The key phrase is “but nineteenth-century writers... should be considered just as integral,” which signals a contrast between the current canon’s focus on post-revolution works and the argument that 19th-century writers are equally important. This implies the blank needs a word suggesting the canon overrepresents the post-1910–1920 period.

steps.title: Match the contrast to the word
steps.content: “Outsized” precisely means larger than appropriate or proportional, capturing how the canon disproportionately includes works from the Mexican Revolution period. The other three options either contradict the contrast (A and B describe neutrality or neglect, which miss the implied excess), or describe inconsistency (C), which is unrelated to the argument. That matches choice D — D is correct.
